mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 8.5% triggers a labor market shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 94.5 triggers a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply chain disruption periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 625,000 triggers a demand shock that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate and a rise in the CPI trigger a monetary policy shock that reduces inflation expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in monetary policy shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in payroll employment above 132,000 triggers a labor market shock that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in labor market shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 133,500 triggers a demand shock that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production and a rise in real GDP trigger a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply chain disruption periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
